channel.title: Cache on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Cache on Pi Stack

item.title: Attic vs Harmonia vs niks3: Best Self-Hosted Nix Binary Cache 2026
item.description: If you are using the Nix package manager across multiple machines, a local binary cache eliminates redundant builds and dramatically speeds up deployments. Instead of every machine compiling the same package from source, you build once, push to your cache server, and all other machines download the pre-built binary.

item.title: Twemproxy vs Mcrouter vs Envoy: Self-Hosted Cache Proxy Guide 2026
item.description: When you run a single Redis or Memcached instance, everything works fine — until it doesn’t. As traffic grows, you need to shard across multiple cache backends, pool connections efficiently, and handle failover without application changes. That’s where a cache proxy sits between your services and your cache layer, transparently managing routing, sharding, and resilience.

item.title: Docker Registry Proxy Cache: Distribution vs Harbor vs Zot 2026
item.description: If you run Docker containers across multiple hosts or CI/CD pipelines, pulling images from Docker Hub repeatedly is wasteful. You hit rate limits, waste bandwidth, and slow down deployments. A self-hosted Docker registry proxy cache solves all three problems.

item.title: Valkey vs DragonflyDB vs Garnet: Best Redis Alternatives in 2026 (Docker Setup)
item.description:
Why You Need a Redis Alternative in 2026

When Redis changed its license to dual SSPL/BSDL in March 2024, the open-source community responded with a wave of alternatives. Two years later, the landscape has matured into three serious contenders — each with a fundamentally different architecture:
